name: Clarke Creek Wind Farm
notable: Staged build: 1,135 MW total. Stage 1 (439 MW) commissioning, Stage 2 (696 MW) development.

timeline.title: Financial close — A$1.5B
timeline.detail: Lacour Energy/Squadron Energy reached financial close

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Construction began for 66 GE Cypress 5.7 MW turbines + 33 GE 3.8 MW

timeline.title: COD estimate updated
timeline.detail: COD changed from 2025-09 to 2025-09-30 (source: AEMO Generation Information)

eis_specs.document_title: Clarke Creek Wind Farm EIS — Coordinator-General's Report (QLD DSDILGP)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Approximate — design envelope; final turbine model confirmed as Goldwind ~4.5 MW class per supplier records
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height from WAsP modelling; Central Queensland near Moranbah
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling from on-site met masts; P50 net yield
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Clarke Creek 132 kV substation (new on-site), stepped up to Powerlink 275 kV at Boomer 275 kV substation
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~55 km of new 132 kV line to Powerlink Boomer 275 kV substation in Central QLD. Long connection required due to greenfield Central QLD location.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: IMPORTANT: Significant new transmission required — ~55 km of 132 kV line plus step-up to Powerlink 275 kV network at Boomer substation. Powerlink augmentation of Boomer substation required. High connection capex for Central QLD location versus Southern QLD wind farms.
eis_specs.notes: 450 MW (100 × ~4.5 MW Goldwind turbines), Central Queensland near Moranbah. Developer: Squadron Energy Services (formerly ACCIONA held interest). QLD EIS approved by Coordinator-General 2020. Commissioning 2024. Central QLD location has moderate wind resource compared to Southern QLD. 132 kV connection voltage is the key constraint — this is unusual for a 450 MW project and means higher transmission losses than 275 kV alternatives. Long 55 km connection distance to Powerlink network adds material capex vs Southern QLD projects.
